system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoSimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      decomposeParDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

numberOfSubdomains  4;

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet straight section (x: -50 to 0, y: 25 to -25)
    // Bottom face (y = -25)
    ( -50 -25 -25 )   // 0
    (   0 -25 -25 )   // 1
    (   0 -25  25 )   // 2
    ( -50 -25  25 )   // 3

    // Top face (y = 25)
    ( -50  25 -25 )   // 4
    (   0  25 -25 )   // 5
    (   0  25  25 )   // 6
    ( -50  25  25 )   // 7

    // Block 1 - upper bend arm (x: 0 to -100, y: 25 to -25)
    ( -100 -25 -25 )  // 8
    ( -100 -25  25 )  // 9
    ( -100  25 -25 )  // 10
    ( -100  25  25 )  // 11

    // Block 2 - outlet straight section (x: -100 to 0, y: -75 to -25)
    ( -100 -75 -25 )  // 12
    (   0 -75 -25 )   // 13
    (   0 -75  25 )   // 14
    ( -100 -75  25 )  // 15
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section (x: -50 to 0, y: -25 to 25)
    // Vertices 0,1,2,3 at y=-25 and 4,5,6,7 at y=25
    // Bottom face (0,1,2,3) winds from +z to -y to -x to +y = CCW when viewed from -x
    // Top face (4,5,6,7) winds from +z to -y to -x to +y = CCW when viewed from +x
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Upper bend arm (x: 0 to -100, y: -25 to 25)
    // This block shares face 4,5,6,7 with Block 0
    // Bottom face (4,5,6,7) is shared and already verified
    // Top face (8,9,10,11) at x=-100
    // For face 8->9->10->11: normal = (0,0,-100) pointing in -x, CCW when viewed from +x ✓
    hex (4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Outlet straight section (x: -100 to 0, y: -75 to -25)
    // Bottom face (12,13,14,15) at y=-75
    // Check face 12->13->14->15: (0,0,100) pointing +z, CCW when viewed from -z ✓
    // Shared face with Block 1: vertices 8,9,10,11
    // For Block 2, this face is 8->11->10->12. Normal = (0,0,-100) pointing -x, CCW when viewed from +x ✓
    hex (12 13 14 11 8 9 10 15) (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 7 4);  // y = 25 face, view from -x: 0->3(+y), 3->7(-z), 7->4(-y), 4->0(+z) = CCW ✓
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (12 11 15 13);  // y = -75 face, view from -x: 12->11(+y), 11->15(-z), 15->13(+y), 13->12(-z) = CW ✗
            // Actually need to check: 12(-50,-75,-25)->11(-100,-75,25)=(-50,+z), 11->15(0,+z), 15->13(+y), 13->12(-y). View from -x: i component negative. 12->11 is -x, so view from +x. 12->11(-x,+z), 11->15(0,+z), 15->13(+y), 13->12(-y). That's +x,+z,+y,-y. Right-hand rule: fingers -x to +z to +y, thumb points -x × +z = +y, then +y × +y = 0. Not CCW. Need 12->13->15->11.
            // Let me recalculate: 12(-100,-75,-25), 13(0,-75,-25), 15(-100,-75,25), 11(-100,-75,25). Face 12->13->15->11: 12->13(+x), 13->15(-x,+z), 15->11(0,+z), 11->12(-z). View from -x: 12->13 is +x (away), so view from +x. 12->13(+x), 13->15(-x,+z), 15->11(0,+z), 11->12(-z). That's +x,-x,+z,+z. Better: 12->13(+x), 13->15 vector from 13 to 15 is (-100,0,0)? No, 13(0,-75,-25) to 15(-100,-75,25) is (-100,0,50). Cross product (13-12)×(15-13) = (100,0,0)×(-100,0,50) = (0*50-0*0, 0*(-100)-100*50, 100*0-0*(-100)) = (0,-5000,0) pointing -y. When viewing from -x, -y is to my right, which means clockwise. So 12->13->15->11 is CW when viewed from -x. Need CCW: 12->11->15->13.
            // 12->11 is (0,0,50), 11->15 is (0,0,-50)... let me think differently. For CCW when viewed from -x, the face vertices in local coordinates (relative to -x viewer) should go up, right, down, left. Vertex 11 is at z=25 (above), 12 at z=-25 (below). So 12(below)->11(above)->15(above other side)->13(below other side). 12->11(+z), 11->15(0,0,-50)? No, 11(-100,-75,25) to 15(-100,-75,25) is same point. I need to pick proper corners.
            // Let me use 12,13,15,11 but reverse: 12->11->15->13.
            // 12(-100,-75,-25) to 11(-100,-75,25) = (0,0,50), 11 to 15 = (0,0,0)... let me use 12,13,14,15 with 14 at (0,-75,25). 12->13(+x), 13->14(-100,0,50), 14->15(-100,0,-50), 15->12(0,0,-50). This is messy. Simpler: use 12,13,14,15 and verify 12->13->14->15 is CCW when viewed from -x.
            // Positional check: 12(-100,-75,-25), 13(0,-75,-25), 14(0,-75,25), 15(-100,-75,25). Looking from -x, so +x axis points right. 12 is at x=-100 (left), 13 at x=0 (right). 12->13 moves right along x. At y=-75, 12 is at z=-25 (down), 13 at z=-25 (down), 14 at z=25 (up), 15 at z=25 (up). So 12(left,bottom) -> 13(right,bottom) -> 14(right,top) -> 15(left,top). Up, right, down, left = CCW. Yes!
            (12 13 14 15);
        );
    }

    upperWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 5 1);  // x = -50 face
            (7 6 2 3);  // x = 0 face (shared with interior)
            (4 8 9 5);  // y = 25 face
            (7 3 1 2);  // z = 25 face
            (8 10 11 9); // z = -25 face
        );
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (1 5 6 2);  // z = 25 face
            (0 3 7 4);  // y = -25 face
            (1 8 9 5);  // x = -100 face (shared with interior)
        );
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 8 4);  // z = -25 face
            (1 2 10 8); // y = -25 face
        );
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 11 2); // z = 25 face
            (0 4 9 3);  // y = 25 face
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: `Block hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) ... is inside-out` — The first block's vertex ordering produces an inside-out hexahedron.
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cannot find "points" — cascading from blockMesh failure.

## Root Cause

The previous 7 attempts have all focused exclusively on fixing vertex ordering within the existing 4-block topology. While vertex ordering fixes have worked for individual blocks temporarily, the **fundamental problem is the block topology itself**. The current design tries to connect 4 blocks at complex junctions where the shared faces don't form proper planar quadrilaterals, creating non-convex or degenerate hexahedra.

## New Approach: Restructure to 3 Blocks with Verified Convex Geometry

The domain should be split into just **3 simple convex blocks**:

### Block 0: Inlet Straight Section (x = -50 to x = 0, y = -25 to y = 25)
```
hex (3 2 1 0 7 6 5 4)
```
- Back face (0,1,2,3) at x=-50: verifies CCW when viewed from -x
- Front face (4,5,6,7) at x=0: verifies CCW when viewed from +x

### Block 1: Upper Bend Arm (x = 0 to x = 25, y = -25 to y = 0)
```
hex (7 11 10 6 3 15 14 2)
```
- The shared face with Block 0 uses vertices (2,3,7,6). For Block 0 with ordering `(3 2 1 0 7 6 5 4)`, this face winds 2→1? Actually, with Block 0 as `(3 2 1 0 7 6 5 4)`, the face at the top is vertices 2,6,7,3. For Block 1, the shared face should be 2→3→15→14. Verify this matches.

### Block 2: Lower Bend/Transition (x = 25 to x = -100, y = -75 to y = -25)
```
hex (12 13 14 15 16 17 18 19)
```

**Critical Fix**: Every boundary face definition must have its vertex ordering explicitly reversed to match the final block orientations. Don't rely on inherited ordering — specify each face with vertices ordered so that the outward normal follows the right-hand rule.

### Boundary Face Ordering Convention

For every boundary face, when viewed from **outside** the domain, the vertices should trace **counter-clockwise**:
- **inlet** (face at y=25): vertices should go from (+x,-z) → (+x,+z) → (-x,+z) → (-x,-z) = `(0 3 7 4)` → use `(4 7 3 0)`
- **outlet** (face at y=-75): vertices should go from (-x,-z) → (-x,+z) → (+x,+z) → (+x,-z) = `(12 15 11 13)` → use `(13 11 15 12)`
- All wall faces: verify using the right-hand rule with your vertex positions

### Summary

1. **Redefine the 3 hex blocks** with verified correct winding (use the tutorial's proven block topology with user's vertex coordinates substituted).
2. **Explicitly reverse every boundary face** so that when viewed from outside, vertices go counter-clockwise.
3. Ensure all boundary patch names in `blockMeshDict` match those in the `0/` field files.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 1.0.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='system/blockMeshDict', changes='Replace entire file with a corrected 3-block topology: Block 0 is inlet straight section hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) with vertices at x=-50..0, y=-25..25, z=-25..25; Block 1 is upper bend arm hex (4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11) connecting to Block 0 at face 4 5 6 7; Block 2 is outlet transition hex (12 13 14 15 16 17 18 19) connecting appropriately; All boundary faces must have CCW vertex ordering when viewed from outside: inlet face (0 3 7 4) becomes (4 7 3 0), outlet face (12 13 14 15) becomes (12 15 14 13), upperWall face (0 4 5 1) becomes (1 5 4 0), lowerWall face (1 2 6 5) becomes (5 6 2 1), leftWall face (0 1 5 4) becomes (4 5 1 0), rightWall face (3 7 11 2) becomes (2 11 7 3); Add mergePatchPairs if needed; Keep scale 1 and all vertex coordinates as user specified')]
